data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Constellation Brands beats Q4 consensus but guides FY27 earnings below estimates
Catalyst Bancorp to acquire Lakeside in $41M all-cash deal
data.description:
• Catalyst Bancorp (CLST) will acquire Lakeside Bancshares (LKSB) in an all-cash transaction valued at ~$41.1M.
  • Lakeside shareholders will receive $19.58 per share in cash.
  • The deal includes merging Lakeside into Catalyst and Lakeside Bank into Catalyst Bank, with Catalyst entities surviving.
  • The combined company will have ~$627M in assets, $400M in loans, and $470M in deposits.
  • The acquisition is expected to more than double Catalyst’s size and significantly boost earnings potential.
  • The transaction is projected to be over 180% accretive to EPS and accretive to tangible book value within 3 years.
  • No additional capital is required, and post-merger capital ratios are expected to remain strong.
  • The deal has board approval and is expected to close in Q3 2026.

data.title: Brookdale March occupancy improves in March; Q1 occupancy gains Y/Y
data.description:
Brookdale's (BKD) occupancy improved in March, the operator of senior living communities said on Wednesday.

Month-end occupancy rose to 83.3% in March, up a tick from 83.2% in February and 80.9% in March 2025. 

Same-community weighted average occupancy was 82.5% in March, compared with 82.7% in February and 81.2% in March 2025.

For Q1 2026, weighted average occupancy grew 280 basis points year-over-year to 82.1%, but slipped 40 bps from Q4 2025. Historically, Brookdale's (BKD) Q1 occupancy has declined by an average of 60 bps from the prior quarter, reflecting such factors as winter weather, illnesses, rate changes, and holidays.

The company historically has its strongest occupancy growth period of the year from May through September. 

Brookdale (BKD) stock ticked down 0.1% in after-hours trading.

data.title: Trump administration considers punishing some NATO allies - report
data.description:
The Trump administration is weighing a plan to relocate U.S. troops away from NATO countries that opposed the Iran war effort, the Wall Street Journal reported Wednesday, citing administration officials.

The proposal would move U.S. forces from nations deemed unhelpful and station them in more supportive allied countries. While falling short of Trump’s recent threats to fully withdraw from NATO—something he cannot do without Congressional approval—the plan signals deepening tensions between Washington and European allies.

“It’s quite sad that NATO turned their backs on the American people over the last six weeks when it’s the American people who have been funding their defense,” White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt said Wednesday.

Several allies have drawn administration ire. Spain blocked U.S. planes from its airspace, Italy briefly denied access to an air base in Sicily and German officials criticized the war publicly. The plan could include closing bases in Spain or Germany.

Eastern European nations viewed as supportive—including Poland, Romania, Lithuania, and Greece—could receive additional troops, the WSJ reported. These countries maintain some of the highest defense spending rates in the alliance and quickly approved U.S. requests for base access.

The White House didn't immediately respond to Seeking Alpha's request for comnent.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Northrop Grumman launches U.S. Space Force satellites on Minotaur IV rocket
data.description:
Northrop Grumman (NOC) on Wednesday said it successfully launched multiple satellites for the U.S. Space Force as part of the STP-S29A mission, using its Minotaur IV rocket from Space Launch Complex 8 at Vandenberg Space Force Base in California.

 

The mission marks the 31st successful flight of the Minotaur program since its debut in 2000, according to the company.

 

Upgraded rocket design

The Minotaur IV vehicle is part of a broader family of launch systems developed for U.S. government missions. It uses decommissioned Peacekeeper missile motors supplied by the government, combined with Northrop Grumman’s commercial launch technologies.

The company said recent design changes to the Minotaur IV are intended to simplify manufacturing and mission preparation. These updates reduce the number of components and processes required, while also improving overall performance and enabling faster launch readiness.

Focus on cost, national security missions

The launch supports the U.S. Space Force’s Rocket Systems Launch Program, which is designed to provide flexible and relatively low-cost access to space for government payloads.

Wes Collier, vice president, launch systems, Northrop Grumman (NOC), said in a statement that the mission reflects ongoing efforts to refine the Minotaur IV system and maintain reliable launch capabilities for national security needs, while continuing to adjust the vehicle’s design for future missions.

data.title: PesoRama to raise up to $7 million in private placement
data.description:
• PesoRama (PESO:CA) on Wednesday said that it plans to raise up to $7 million through a non-brokered private placement to fund store expansion and general corporate purposes.
  • The company intends to issue up to 20 million units at $0.35 each, with each unit comprising one common share and half a warrant. 
  • Each full warrant will be exercisable at $0.50 per share for 36 months.
  • PesoRama said it may accelerate the warrant expiry if its shares trade at or above $0.75 for 10 consecutive trading days.
  • Canaccord Genuity will act as a finder for the offering, with potential fees of up to 7% in cash.
  • The company will also pay $25,000 to Markette Ventures for use of its digital investment platform.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about April 21, subject to regulatory approvals, with the securities subject to a four-month holding period.
  • CCORF closed +2.95% at $8.71.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Huachen AI Parking to implement 1-for-30 reverse stock split
data.description:
• Huachen AI Parking (HCAI) on Wednesday said that it will implement a 1-for-30 reverse stock split of its Class A and Class B shares, effective April 13, subject to Nasdaq approval.
  • The company said the move is intended to increase its share price to help maintain compliance with Nasdaq’s minimum bid requirement. Its Class A shares will continue to trade under the ticker “HCAI.”
  • Following the split, every 30 shares will be consolidated into one, reducing Class A shares outstanding to about 629,167 from roughly 18.9 million and Class B shares to about 533,333 from 16 million.
  • The par value of both share classes will increase to $0.0000375, and the total number of authorized shares will be reduced accordingly, the company said.
  • Fractional shares will be rounded up to the nearest whole share.
  • The reverse split was authorized by shareholders in December, with the board approving the 1-for-30 ratio on March 24.
  • The company will also adopt a new CUSIP number, while retaining its existing ticker symbol.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Northrop Grumman partners with 4iG on Hungary’s first sovereign communications satellite
data.description:
Northrop Grumman (NOC) and 4iG Space and Defence Technologies on Tuesday said they began work on a geosynchronous satellite that will serve as Hungary’s first domestically controlled communications platform.

 

 

The satellite is expected to provide secure Ka-band connectivity for both government and commercial users across the region, marking a step toward greater national control over critical communications infrastructure.

GEOStar-3 platform selected for mission

The spacecraft will be based on Northrop Grumman’s GEOStar-3 platform, a widely used satellite bus that has supported more than 50 missions globally.

The platform is commonly deployed for both military and commercial communications systems, offering a standardized architecture designed for geosynchronous orbit operations.

Subheading: Agreement builds on earlier cooperation framework
The project follows a previously signed memorandum of understanding between the two companies covering broader collaboration in space and defense programs.

Company representatives indicated the partnership combines Northrop Grumman’s manufacturing capabilities with 4iG’s regional role, with the goal of delivering a system aligned with Hungary’s security and communications needs.

Subheading: Focus on secure regional connectivity
The satellite is intended to support secure data transmission across Central and Eastern Europe, reflecting growing demand among governments for independent and resilient communications systems.

Once deployed, the system will provide Hungary with direct access to satellite-based communications capacity, reducing reliance on third-party infrastructure while enabling expanded commercial services.

data.title: Universal Logistics names new CFO
data.description:
• Universal Logistics (ULH) appointed Michael Rogers as chief financial officer and treasurer effective June 1, 2026.
  • Rogers currently serves as CFO of Conlan Tire Co. and affiliates and previously spent ~30 years at Ford Motor Company in senior finance roles.
  • He will succeed Jude M. Beres, who is resigning effective May 29, 2026.
  • Beres will remain through late May to support a smooth leadership transition.

data.title: Boeing delivers ViaSat-3 satellite for Asia-Pacific ahead of SpaceX launch
data.description:
Boeing (BA) on Tuesday said it delivered the ViaSat-3 Flight 3 satellite to Viasat, marking a key step in a program aimed at expanding high-capacity communications coverage to the Asia-Pacific region.

 

The spacecraft, built at Boeing’s El Segundo facility in California, will now undergo pre-launch processing at Cape Canaveral Space Force Station in Florida. A launch aboard a SpaceX (SPACE) Falcon Heavy rocket is planned, with Boeing and Viasat supporting final preparations.

 

Program milestone completes Boeing’s production work

The delivery represents the completion of Boeing’s role in manufacturing and integrating the third satellite in the ViaSat-3 constellation.

Executives from both companies described the handoff as a significant point in the program. Boeing’s (BA) satellite systems leadership indicated the spacecraft reflects the maturity of its 702 platform and the ongoing collaboration with Viasat. Viasat’s space systems leadership said the satellite is expected to play an important role in providing secure and adaptable connectivity across the region.

Satellite designed for high-demand bandwidth markets

ViaSat-3 F3 is intended to support commercial mobility, government and defense users in high-density markets across Asia-Pacific. The satellite is part of a broader constellation designed to deliver global coverage with flexible bandwidth allocation.

The first satellite in the series is already supporting in-flight connectivity services, while the second is progressing toward service over the Americas. The third satellite will extend coverage into Asia-Pacific once operational.

Boeing platform adapted for larger, high-power payloads

The spacecraft is based on Boeing’s 702MP+ platform, an evolution of its 702 satellite family. The design allows for larger payloads and higher power output, supported by expanded solar arrays, increased battery capacity and upgraded electronics.

The satellite also uses all-electric propulsion and incorporates structural and control system enhancements to maintain precise positioning despite its size. Thermal management systems have been upgraded to handle the demands of a high-power mission.

Next step is launch and orbital deployment

With delivery complete, the focus shifts to launch and early orbit operations. Boeing and Viasat will continue working together through launch and deployment, as the companies move toward bringing the satellite into service.

Once operational, ViaSat-3 F3 is expected to complete the constellation’s intended geographic reach, adding capacity in a region where demand for satellite-based connectivity continues to grow.
